assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress

Line 28: 'Concurrent triage detected on the same upstream component (quinn-proto). Another engineer (engineer-b@example.com) is actively triaging TC-8019, which also affects the quinn-proto component.'

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 36-50: The 'Warning Presented to Engineer' section includes a table with 'TC-8019' as the CVE Issue and 'engineer-b@example.com' as the Assignee.

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 46-50: Three numbered options: '1. Wait -- pause until the other triage completes, then re-run Step 4.3 to detect any overlap', '2. Skip -- skip remediation task creation for this CVE', '3. Proceed -- create tasks anyway with a concurrent-triage-overlap label so the other engineer’s Step 4.3 catches the overlap'.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 1: 'Step 7 -- Concurrent Triage Detection' and line 63: 'The engineer must choose one of the three options before the triage can proceed to Step 8 (Remediation).' triage-outcome.md lines 22-29: 'Step 7 Gate: Concurrent Triage' section appears before Case A (line 40) and Case B (line 59) sections, with 'Remediation task creation (below) only proceeds if the engineer selects option 3.'
